name: Secure Software Development Framework
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST SP 800-218 (2022) defines the Secure Software Development Framework — a set of fundamental, sound, and secure software development practices. Companion 800-218A extends it to AI/ML model development (already in repo).
relationToCloudSecurity: Software supply-chain security framework; relevant to cloud-native development pipelines.
relationToAISecurity: AI-specific overlay at SP 800-218A. The base SSDF applies to AI tooling and supporting infrastructure.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: Version 1.1 published 2022-02-04. Required for federal software acquisitions per EO 14028 and OMB M-22-18.

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_218.py parses the SSDF practice/task tables into structured CSV/JSON: 47 tasks across 19 practices in 4 practice groups (Prepare the Organization, Protect the Software, Produce Well-Secured Software, Respond to Vulnerabilities). Each task carries its parent practice context.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire from NIST CSRC and extract.
